title: identity — declared entitlements
description: Five entitlement keys. TOTP + recovery codes are baseline (every tier). WebAuthn, password breach check, and password history are medium+ tier features. Entitlements are enforced by `identity/auth` (which owns the endpoints) but declared here because they are properties of the credential substrate.

entitlements.description: TOTP (RFC 6238) MFA enrolment. Baseline across every plan tier — this is the industry-standard second factor and the security floor Academorix enforces.
WebAuthn (FIDO2) second factor. Requires HTTPS + browser support; harder to bootstrap in dev. Free tier gated off to keep the fallback surface simple; team+ can enrol hardware keys.
Recovery-code fallback for MFA. Always on — without it, losing the primary factor locks the identity out. Ten codes generated at enrolment; regenerable on demand.
haveibeenpwned k-anonymity check on every password set / reset. Free tier gated off to avoid an outbound-API dependency on shoestring deployments; every paid tier ships the check on.
Rolling-window password reuse prevention. Free tier only enforces the current password (no history); team+ enforces `identity.password.history_size` (default 5).
